title: gnaphalium andrewsii
intro: Gnaphalium andrewsii, also known as Western Cudweed, is a perennial plant in the Asteraceae family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Western Cudweed include reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and relieving congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and coughs. Western Cudweed can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in salves and creams to promote skin healing and reduce pain.
